creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Crucial travel info about the Amalfi Coast👇

The Amalfi Coast had long been a bucket list destination, and I’d done a fair bit of research before finally arriving - here are some of the main things I think you should know before travelling to this part of Italy:

𝟲) You need to book your hotel well in advance. No, I mean - WELL in advance. The Amalfi Coast is famous worldwide, but the coast itself is only about 50 kilometres long and it’s not like it’s plastered with hotels (which is nice, of course). 

𝟱) Don’t rent a car unless you enjoy sitting in traffic with an elevated heart rate 😅 Before deciding whether you should rent a car or not; I’d recommend searching for “Amalfi coast traffic” on Youtube. Oh, it’s also impossible to find a place to park in the main towns along the coast. 
𝟰) Use ferries instead of renting a car or taking the bus. So traffic along the Amalfi Coast is mental, but luckily, there are a lot of different ferry companies that basically act like public transportation. The best thing about skipping the roads? You get to see the incredibly beautiful coast from the sea.  𝟯) Book restaurants in advance. Especially if you’re staying in popular tourist towns like Amalfi or Positano. I think you’d be surprised by how small these towns actually are - they may be all over social media, but there aren’t actually that many places to eat and the good ones get booked up every day. Planning ahead when you visit the Amalfi Coast is literally key.
 𝟮) People think they must go through Naples to get to the Amalfi Coast, but you can get from Rome to Salerno in just over 2 hours on a high-speed train. From Salerno, you can get a ferry (or a bus if you enjoy traffic) to the towns along the Amalfi Coast. There’s a link in my bio to the website I use to buy train tickets all over Europe.

𝟭) My controversial opinion is that Sorrento is the most interesting town in the area. While it’s practically not located on the actual Amalfi Coast, Sorrento should be on your radar. It’s bigger than the towns along the Amalfi Coast, so getting an affordable hotel is a bit easier.  Have you been to the Amalfi Coast? What did you think? 🇮🇹 #amalficoast #traveltips
